scoring_method.community_score: 1-10 scale based on total community size
scoring_method.market_validation_score: 1-15 scale: bestselling books + active market revenue + monetization proof + urgency

scored_blue_oceans.notes: 7.75M across r/DnD (4.4M), r/DMAcademy (850K), r/rpg (1.9M). Hasbro D&D revenue $1B+/year. D&D Beyond 13M+ users who play but have NO tool to master DM craft. Matt Colville 'Running the Game' YouTube has 800K subs — proof of intense demand for DM education content. 'The Monsters Know What They're Doing' (Keith Ammann) sold 500K+ on DM strategy alone. Critical Role $20M+ merchandise. D&D grew 300% during COVID. Gap: NO app teaches DM craft one concept per day: encounter pacing, NPC voice differentiation, treasure placement math, tension curves, player motivation types, improvisation frameworks, rule-of-cool adjudication. Cross-sell with scripture-streak infrastructure directly — same streak mechanic.

9.4M across r/leagueoflegends (6M), r/esports (1.2M), r/GlobalOffensive (1.8M), r/valorant (800K). Esports viewership 640M globally. League of Legends 150M+ registered accounts. ProGuides charges $10/mo for video guides and generates $2M+ ARR — the market pays. Riot Games $2B+ annual revenue. Yet ProGuides is video courses, not streak-based daily learning. Gap: no app teaches one macro concept per day (wave management, vision control, draft theory, champion matchups, mental game, team communication) with streak accountability. Premium tier at $7.99/mo would capture 0.1% of 9.4M = $75K MRR at scale.

5.785M across r/boardgames (5.5M), r/boardgamedesign (85K), r/tabletop (200K). Tabletop gaming industry $12B/year globally. BGG 2M+ registered users visit daily. Kickstarter tabletop: $230M+ raised in 2023. No app teaches strategy theory: worker placement efficiency, engine building theory, deck thinning vs power cards, hand management, negotiation tells, information asymmetry exploitation. Adults learning board game theory would pay $4.99/mo premium for credentialed strategic improvement.

2.28M across r/poker (2.1M), r/pokerstrategy (100K), r/livepoker (80K). Online poker $8B/year industry. 'The Theory of Poker' (Sklansky) sold 500K+ and created modern poker education. 'Thinking in Bets' (Annie Duke) sold 300K+ — proof general population wants poker-adjacent decision theory. GTO solvers (GTO+, PioSOLVER) require expert proficiency — no onboarding path. Gap: daily micro-lesson app teaching pot odds, range construction, exploitative adjustments, ICM concepts, bankroll math. Poker players are accustomed to paying for coaching ($50-200/hr). App at $9.99/mo is trivially cheap by comparison.

2.13M across r/gamedev (1.5M), r/gamedesign (250K), r/indiegames (380K). Unity 5M+ registered devs. 'The Art of Game Design' (Schell) sold 150K+ and is on 200+ university syllabi. Indie game market $3B+/year. GDC attendance 30K/year at $1,000+ per ticket — these people pay to learn. Gap: no daily micro-lesson app teaching feedback loops, core loop design, progression curve math, onboarding funnel design, UI/UX for games, monetization psychology, feel and juice. Aspiring game designers would pay $6.99/mo to build knowledge daily.

2.24M (r/rpg 1.9M, Pathfinder 200K, CoC 100K, BitD 40K). TTRPG industry $450M/year growing 15%/yr. Pathfinder 2e launched to acclaim. Blades in the Dark won multiple awards and created the 'Forged in the Dark' design ecosystem. Knowledge is fragmented across hundreds of systems. Gap: cross-system mechanics education — dice probability, encounter balance, narrative frames, safety tools (Lines & Veils, X-Card), system design theory. Advanced GMs pay for educational content.

2.2M across r/retrogaming (1.6M), r/SNES (200K), r/NES (100K), r/GameBoy (200K), r/atari (100K). NES Classic sold 1.5M units immediately at $60 — strongest nostalgia demand signal possible. 'Console Wars' (Harris) sold 200K+. 'Masters of Doom' (Kushner) sold 500K+. Gaming Historian YouTube 500K subs, Did You Know Gaming 3M+ subs with no app equivalent. Gap: daily gaming history lesson — one event, company, person, or game per day with context, significance, and how it changed the industry.

1.05M across r/warhammer40k (650K), r/ageofsigmar (100K), r/WarhammerFantasy (100K), r/totalwar (200K). Games Workshop £426M annual revenue. Black Library 2M+ books/year. Total War: Warhammer series 15M+ copies. Warhammer+ streaming launched 2021. The lore is 35+ years deep and impenetrable to new fans — perfect educational gap. App teaching one lore fact per day (factions, events, characters, technology, theology) would serve both veterans deepening knowledge and newcomers onboarding to the hobby.

780K across r/puzzles (310K), r/riddles (300K), r/logicpuzzles (80K), r/mathpuzzles (90K). Wordle sold to NYT for ~$1M — a simple free word puzzle. NYT Games 1M+ paid subscribers. 'Lateral Thinking Puzzles' series (Paul Sloane) sold 1M+ total. Gap: app teaching puzzle-solving FRAMEWORKS daily (not just serving puzzles): lateral thinking techniques, constraint satisfaction, Occam's Razor application, deductive chain building, insight problem solving patterns. Different from Duolingo/Lumosity — teaching meta-skills, not practicing exercises.

410K across r/speedrun (230K), r/speedrunning (80K), GDQ community (100K). GDQ 2024 raised $3.6M for charity — proof of community spending power. 2M+ runs on speedrun.com. Knowledge is locked in game-specific Discord servers and wikis. Gap: teaching the universal science of speedrunning — RNG theory, movement optimization principles, routing logic, glitch taxonomy, split management psychology. Highly engaged niche willing to pay for expertise.

cross_sell_insight: D&D DM Streak and TTRPG Mechanics Streak share 60%+ of target audience — cross-sell stack. Board Game Strategy and Game Design Principles share game-theory mindset users. Esports Meta and Speedrunning Science share optimization-minded gaming community. Three mini-clusters for upsell chains.
